title: God as Creator and Sustainer
intro: In the beginning, God created. But what is less often considered is what came after — He did not step back. He did not wind up the universe and leave it to run on its own. He stayed. He holds. He sustains.
reading:
Genesis 1:1 establishes that God is the source and origin of all that exists — in the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth. Isaiah 40:28 adds that He is the everlasting God, the Creator of the ends of the earth. Psalm 33:6 tells us the heavens were made by the word of His mouth — and that same word continues to uphold them.

John 1:3 makes the scope unmistakable: all things were made through Him, and without Him was not anything made that was made. Colossians 1:16 extends this even further — by Him all things were created, in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible, thrones and dominions, rulers and authorities. Everything.

But Gavin's outline draws our attention to what follows creation: sustaining. Colossians 1:17 says in Him all things hold together — not held together once, but hold together, present tense, continuously. Hebrews 1:3 calls Christ the one who upholds the universe by the word of His power.

Acts 17:28 puts it simply: in Him we live and move and have our being. You are not merely sustained by oxygen and food. You are sustained by God Himself. Every heartbeat is a gift of His ongoing creative power.

The God who spoke galaxies into existence is the same God who is actively holding your life together right now.
reflection: How does it change the way you move through your day to know that God is not just the One who made you, but the One who is actively sustaining you in this very moment?
abide: Creator and Sustainer — in You I live and move and have my being. I did not hold myself together today. You did. I receive that gift with gratitude and rest in the One who holds all things.
